data.body:
Plan-review advisory triage (PASS + signed). The advisories correctly flag that the dispatch-seam extraction is non-trivial and must be verified against the REAL _apply_batch body before extracting:
- T1/G6 (critical): before creating apply_handlers.py, READ applier.py:533-918 and confirm the per-action handlers are cleanly separable. If a handler closes over _apply_batch locals (the batch client, drift state, manifest accumulator, recorder), extract it as a function that takes that state EXPLICITLY as params — do NOT extract in a way that silently shares mutable closure state. Preserve the abort-without-further-Jira-calls-on-drift + idempotent-apply invariants exactly.
- G6 (major): the per-mutation step helper (drift recheck + dispatch + record) must keep the early-abort semantics (return/raise that stops further Jira calls) — verify the control flow translates faithfully when lifted out of the nested loop.
- T7/E1 (major): handle BOTH docs/architecture.md branches deterministically — if applier.py drops <800 LOC, REMOVE it from .github/module-size-allowlist.txt AND remove/My update its offenders-table row; if it stays >=800, update the row to reflect the new dispatcher/handlers split. (The structural module-size test from drag-gripe-brake will enforce allowlist==over-cap set, so the allowlist MUST match reality.)
- F1 (minor): "materially shorter" is judged by the nesting<=4 + the handler LOC moved out; not a separate measurable gate.
Approach: extract handlers with explicit params; add an import/structure test; run the full apply/idempotency/drift-abort suite unchanged. Claim + implement after romp (PR #81) merges (depends_on ff7d).
